title: Rhythm: build muscle memory
description: Rhythm exercise - combine all learned keys in a smooth flowing text
finger_focus: All keys learned - focus on rhythm

tips.text: No new keys today. We hold the line and work on rhythm. Speed comes later; an even keystroke comes first.
Keep your fingers on the home row and your eyes on the screen. Type each word at one steady pace, the way a clock keeps time. Don't rush the last letter.

tips.title: An even hand
tips.text: Listen for a regular click between keys. The same small pause every time, no hurry, no stall. Even keystrokes read like clean copy.

tips.title: Rhythm: she
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: she she she. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: said
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: said said said. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: here
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: here here here. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.text: That steady tempo is the whole point. When the spacing between keys stops wandering, your typing reads smooth, and the words start to look after themselves.
Worked through every step? Open the trainer below and make one full run on the lesson text. That run saves your progress and unlocks the next lesson.

character_tips.anna: Rhythm lesson! Don't chase speed - find a smooth rhythm. Type musically.
character_tips.maxim: Steady pace. Each symbol - one click. Don't rush, don't stall.
character_tips.klavochka: Musically, sunshine! Smoothly, like a dance 💃
